McPhy partners with UNIFI for a joint lab and research agreement in alkaline electrolyzer tech on Dec 19, 2024.
news.summary: On December 19th, 2024, at 5:45 pm CET, in Foussemagne, France, McPhy Energy, a prominent European company specializing in alkaline electrolyzer technology and production, revealed a significant development. They disclosed the initiation of a Joint Laboratory and a collaborative research agreement with the University of Florence (UNIFI) in Italy.

news.headline: Capgemini SE reduces its share capital, details undisclosed.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, which is a significant increase from the contraction of 3.5% in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In terms of GDP, the United States is expected to see a growth of 6.8%, China by 8.4%, and the Eurozone by 4.3%. However, the recovery is not consistent across all countries, with emerging markets facing greater challenges. Advanced economies are forecasted to reach pre-pandemic levels by the end of 2022, while it may take longer for developing countries to fully recover. The report also emphasizes the importance of global cooperation and policy support to ensure a sustainable and inclusive recovery worldwide.

news.headline: Laird Superfood launches Maca Instant Latte for energy and vitality, combining coffee, coconut creamer, mushrooms, and maca root.
news.summary: A recently launched beverage created by Laird Superfood® in Boulder, Colorado blends coffee with coconut creamer, functional mushrooms, and Maca, a root from the Andes Mountains in Peru. The new product is gaining attention for its innovative combination of ingredients. Laird Superfood® is known for its plant-based superfood products and is a prominent player in the market for functional coffee and creamers. This news was announced on December 19, 2024, by PRNewswire. Laird Superfood® is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol LSF.

news.headline: California Credit Union gives 525+ toys to LA Boys & Girls Club for kids, bringing holiday joy.
news.summary: In Glendale, California on December 19, 2024, California Credit Union joined forces with the Los Angeles Boys & Girls Club to spread holiday joy to more than 500 Club children. The partnership saw the credit union, along with its staff and members, donating a total of 525 new toys and gifts. This collaboration aimed to bring much-needed cheer to the young members of the club during the festive season.

news.headline: FLUENT acquires RIV Capital, expanding into NY's cannabis market, strengthening its multi-state presence.
news.summary: Propels FLUENT into a prominent and rapidly expanding cannabis market globally.

news.headline: Bitfinex hacker, Ilya Lichtenstein, defends wife post-sentencing for 2016 hack. avoids blame on money laundering.
news.summary: Ilya Lichtenstein, in a message to his social media audience, requested them not to hold his wife accountable for the 2016 Bitfinex hack, in which they were both allegedly involved in money laundering.

news.headline: ProAmpac's ProActive Recyclable® RP-1000 Paper Series nominated in two categories at PAC Global Awards 2025, used by Marks & Spencer.
news.summary: In Cincinnati, ProAmpac, a prominent player in flexible packaging and material science, has revealed that its ProActive Recyclable® RP-1000 Paper Series has been showcased as a finalist in two categories at the esteemed PAC Global Awards 2025. This highlights ProAmpac's commitment to developing eco-friendly and technologically advanced packaging options. The award-nominated ProActive Recyclable® RP-1000 Paper Series, featured in Marks & Spencer's Select Farms Organic potato range, has garnered significant recognition for its sustainable qualities.

news.headline: Asia-Pacific gypsum-based plasterboard market valued at $7.20 billion in 2023, estimated to grow at 8.31% CAGR, reaching $16.00 billion by 2033.
news.summary: An analysis report titled "Asia-Pacific Gypsum-Based Plasterboard Market and Alternatives: Focus on End-User Application, Type, Material, and Country" has been newly available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The Asia-Pacific gypsum-based plasterboard market and alternatives achieved a value of $7.20 billion in 2023, with projections suggesting a growth at a CAGR of 8.31%. The market is expected to reach $16.00 billion by 2033. The report focuses on gypsum-based plasterboard and its various substitutes, offering insights into the industry trends and potential opportunities.

news.headline: Ryze Labs selects first group for AI Combinator, fostering crypto-AI innovation. Launch aimed at top Web3 projects.
news.summary: Ryze Labs has initiated its AI Combinator program to promote innovation where crypto and AI intersect. The program has welcomed its inaugural batch of projects, marking a significant milestone for the organization. As a renowned investor in cutting-edge Web3 ventures, Ryze Labs' move showcases its commitment to fostering creativity in this niche space. The first group of projects selected for this program will have the opportunity to leverage Ryze Labs' expertise and resources to further develop their initiatives. This development comes at a time when the intersection of crypto and AI is gaining momentum, highlighting the importance of initiatives such as the AI Combinator program in pushing the boundaries of technological advancement. Stay updated to witness how these projects will contribute to the evolving landscape of innovation.

news.headline: Nomios integrates Qevlar AI into its 7 SOCs, benefiting 67 European clients at no extra cost, improving teams' efficiency.
news.summary: Paris - Nomios, a prominent European cybersecurity company, has revealed the incorporation of Qevlar's generative AI into its Security Operations Centers (SOCs). After a focused assessment period, which notably enhanced team productivity, the Qevlar AI system is now seamlessly operational in all seven managed SOCs of Nomios. Notably, all 67 European customers can access this enhanced service at no additional charge. Nomios launched its Modern SOC four years ago, and this integration marks a significant advancement for the company and its clients.

news.headline: Sonic and Injective partner to create cross-chain AI Agent platform, merging Solana and Injective capabilities for developing AI agents across blockchain ecosystems through Smart Agent Hub. Integration enables execution of Solana smart contracts on Injective, aided by Sonic's HyperGrid technology. Communication between networks facilitated through IBC, supporting seamless asset transfer. (Character count: 293)
news.summary: Sonic and Injective have collaborated to create the industry's first cross-chain AI Agent platform, known as the Smart Agent Hub. This partnership combines Solana's capabilities with Injective's to enhance AI agent development. Through Sonic's HyperGrid technology, a dedicated Solana Virtual Machine grid is established within Injective, enabling direct communication between the two networks via the Inter-Blockchain Communication Protocol. This integration permits the execution of Solana smart contracts on Injective, fostering interoperability. An essential feature of this collaboration is the bidirectional bridge for seamless asset transfer including SOL and Injective tokens.

news.headline: Vantiva to sell Supply Chain Solutions Division to Variant Equity-managed funds.
Dec. 19, 2024: AAHRPP accredits University of North Texas Health Science Center/North Texas Regional IRB for research protection.
news.summary: The University of North Texas Health Science Center / North Texas Regional Institutional Review Board has been granted full accreditation by the Association for the Accreditation of Human Research Protection Programs in Washington on December 19, 2024. This recognition highlights the board's commitment to ensuring the ethical conduct of research involving human subjects. The IRB is responsible for reviewing and overseeing research projects to protect the rights and welfare of participants. This accreditation further solidifies the institution's dedication to upholding the highest standards in research practices.

Downey's Potato Chips partners with Meijer, adds 39 SE Michigan stores. Michigan company showcased in 'Local Endcap' program.
news.summary: A program called 'Local Endcap' by Meijer is highlighting Michigan-based companies in Waterford, Michigan. On December 19, 2024, Downey's Potato Chips, a small batch kettle cooked chips producer from Michigan, revealed its inclusion in 39 Meijer stores across Southeast Michigan as part of the initiative.

news.headline: Hyundai IONIQ 5 N named Best EV for 2025 by CarBuzz, boasting 601 horsepower and a N Grin Boost function.
news.summary: CarBuzz has announced that the IONIQ 5 N is impressively thrilling, akin to top-tier performance vehicles. The all-electric model boasts 601 horsepower, with a driver-selectable N Grin Boost1 function adding 40 extra horsepower. The N version takes precedence over M, setting a benchmark in the electric vehicle sector.

news.headline: Big Lots no longer closing sale to Nexus Capital Management, pursuing going concern transaction. Preparing for going out of business sales at remaining stores.
news.summary: Big Lots, based in Columbus, Ohio, has announced that they no longer expect to finalize the sale to Nexus Capital Management as previously indicated. Instead, the company is focusing on a going concern transaction. They are now preparing to start conducting going out of business sales at their remaining stores. This decision comes after efforts to complete the sale fell through. The development was shared on December 19, 2024, and it marks a change in strategy for the company as they shift their focus to ensuring the continuity of their operations.

news.headline: WBC suggests Santa move workshop to Chicago for space, spirit, and innovation. - Chicago, Dec 19, 2024.
news.summary: "Embracing the holiday season, World Business Chicago (WBC) showcases Chicago as the ideal business destination for global CEOs like Santa Claus. With ample space, vibrant spirit, and a culture of innovation, Chicago stands out as a thriving hub for businesses. This initiative aims to attract more business opportunities to the city. Chicago, on December 19, 2024, comes alive with the festive appeal and economic promise as WBC presents its annual pitch to global business leaders, emphasizing the city's strengths. The campaign includes a charming illustration highlighting the city's appeal, making a strong case for companies seeking growth opportunities. Through this approach, WBC targets to boost Chicago's economic prosperity, with the hope of creating a favorable environment for business growth. By showcasing its unique assets and potential, Chicago aspires to become a top choice for CEOs seeking expansion and success in their ventures."

news.headline: NeuroBlade partners with Amazon EC2 F2 instances using AMD FPGA and EPYC CPU, enhancing data analytics speed.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, in Mountain View, California, NeuroBlade, a top player in data analytics acceleration, revealed its partnership with the latest Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) F2 instances. These instances are backed by AMD FPGA and EPYC CPU technologies. This collaboration signifies a major step in data analytics acceleration, combining NeuroBlade's expertise with Amazon's cutting-edge cloud computing platform.

news.headline: FactSet Research Systems reported Q1 2025 sales of $568.7M, beating estimates, with adjusted EPS of $4.37. Annual Subscription Value was $2.27B.
news.summary: In the first quarter of fiscal 2025, FactSet Research Systems Inc (NYSE: FDS) recorded a sales growth of 4.9% year-on-year, reaching $568.7 million, surpassing the analyst consensus of $565.13 million. Organic revenues also saw a 4.7% increase to $567.7 million. The growth in GAAP and Organic revenues was primarily fueled by wealth, asset owners, and institutional asset managers. The adjusted EPS stood at $4.37, beating the analyst consensus of $4.26. The company reported an Annual Subscription Value (ASV) of $2.27 billion, out of which organic ASV accounted for $2.26 billion. The strong operational performance in the fiscal year was attributed to sustained cost discipline. For further details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Nana Grants revives CAPS Bridge Funding Program for low-income single mothers with $500,000 from The Liz Blake Giving Fund and $25,000 from Gas South.
news.summary: A nonprofit organization called Nana Grants is reintroducing its CAPS Bridge Funding Program after a four-year break due to increased demand. The program covers child care costs for low-income single mothers pursuing college education. The Liz Blake Giving Fund and Gas South have committed $500,000 and $25,000 respectively to support this initiative.

news.summary: During her recent interview following the podcast's rise to the top spot, she reveals insights into the reasons behind the show's sudden success.

news.headline: Ethereum's price drops 5% to $3,612.85, sparking concerns about recovery. A whale made $83 million profit from ETH trades.
news.summary: Ethereum (ETH) experienced a 5% decline on Thursday, causing speculation about its potential to surpass the critical $3,700 mark by the end of the year. Lookonchain data revealed a notable whale linked to LonglingCapital, who bought 6,000 ETH during the dip and has earned $83 million in profits due to expert timing. Since May 8, 2023, the whale has accumulated 75,400 ETH (valued at $180.4 million with an average cost of $2,392) and sold 50,800 ETH (worth $172.8 million with an average price of $3,401). As of now, Ethereum is priced at $3,612.85 with a market cap of $435.1 billion, showing a 6.7% decrease in the last 24 hours and an 8.9% drop in the past week. Bitcoin (BTC) is currently valued at $100,537.58 with a market cap of $1.98 trillion, experiencing a 3.7% decrease in the last 24 hours and a 1.3% decrease in the past week. XRP is priced at $2.32 with a market cap of $132.7 billion, showing a 6.1% decline in the last 24 hours and a 4.1% decrease in the past week. Notably, trader MaxBecauseBTC outlined two scenarios in a post on X, offering valuable insights for other traders.

news.headline: The Osmolality Lab in Utah tests personal lubricants, found most fail WHO standards. #osmolality #DRAPER #PRLab
news.summary: A recent study found that the majority of lubricants currently available do not meet the World Health Organization's recommended osmolality level. The Osmolality Lab, LLC, based in DRAPER, Utah, is leading the way in testing the osmolality of personal care products. Osmolality is a crucial factor in ensuring the safety and efficacy of lubricants, with inadequate levels potentially causing harm. This research sheds light on the importance of proper osmolality levels in these products.

news.headline: Mission Critical Partners to assess Columbus's alternative response capabilities for incidents beyond traditional services.
news.summary: Mission Critical Partners (MCP) based in State College, Pennsylvania, announced on December 19, 2024, that they will analyze the methods adopted by Columbus, Ohio, to deliver 911 service and emergency response for incidents beyond conventional law enforcement, fire/rescue, and emergency medical services. The firm will closely examine the strategies to improve these essential services.

news.headline: GarageFloorCoating.com provides waterproofing services for Nashville basements to combat high humidity issues, protecting homes from moisture damage.
news.summary: Nashville, being notorious for its high humidity and erratic weather conditions, poses a significant challenge for residents. The underground areas in the city are especially susceptible to moisture issues. Failure to waterproof basements can lead to the accumulation of humidity, leading to extensive damage to homes. Therefore, it is crucial for residents to address this concern to safeguard their homes.

news.headline: Boston Children's Museum receives $2.5M grant from Lilly Endowment to foster empathy in children and support character development.

The Boston Children's Museum in Boston has recently been granted $2,499,243 from Lilly for this five-year project. This initiative seeks to emphasize the importance of empathy in character development among children and will work towards empowering adults to assist in this process.

news.headline: Urgent need for improved morning sickness services highlighted; calls for better diagnosis and treatment options in pregnancy.
news.summary: The medical field is urging for improved diagnosis methods and treatment choices for pregnancy-induced nausea/vomiting and hyperemesis gravidarum. A specialized women's health platform, Harmonia Healthcare, emphasizes addressing these conditions that are often overlooked and inadequately managed. This call comes as cases of hyperemesis gravidarum have increased by 20% in the last year, affecting approximately 2% of pregnant individuals. The medical community highlights the urgency of advancements in managing these challenging aspects of pregnancy to ensure better outcomes for expectant mothers.

news.summary: Many homes in New Jersey have basements that face harsh conditions including heavy rains, high humidity, and fluctuating temperatures. These factors lead to moisture issues, affecting basements first.
